---
title: "Dense-flowered Smartweed (Persicaria glabra)"
slug: "persicaria-glabra"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180778698/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Dense-flowered Smartweed"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Caryophyllales"
tax_family: "Polygonaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Caryophyllales", "Polygonaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180778698/medium.jpg" alt="Dense-flowered Smartweed" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/lucieb30">
lucieb30</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/107478839">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Caryophyllales
-   **Family:** Polygonaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=2000263)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats such as marshes, pond margins, and ditches.

## Natural history notes

A wetland plant characterized by its tall, branching stems, lance-shaped leaves, and dense spikes of small white to pinkish flowers. In Florida, it is commonly found in moist to wet habitats such as marshes, pond margins, and ditches. It grows during the warmer months and often forms stands in shallow water or along wetland edges, providing habitat for insects and other aquatic organisms.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/200026-Persicaria-glabra)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3868)
